title: quercus infectoria subsp. infectoria
intro: Quercus infectoria subsp. infectoria, also known as galls of oak, is a plant-based product derived from the oak tree Quercus infectoria, which produces galls that are rich in tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Quercus infectoria subsp. infectoria include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus infectoria subsp. infectoria include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. Quercus infectoria subsp. infectoria is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or extract, and is used in traditional medicine in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and teas.
